zh.Stowell, Somerset, England, United Kingd: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特斯度維爾
zh.Stawell, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特斯塔維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Hody於從1330年到1332年出生在英國哈普爾時，他的父親Richard Hody23歲，母親Margaret Wormegay17歲。他和Margaret Bardolph至少有2個兒子。他去世於1372年在英國薩默塞特斯塔維爾，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.Stawell, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特斯塔維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Hody于从1330年到1332年出生在英国哈普爾时，他的父亲Richard Hody23岁，母亲Margaret Wormegay17岁。他和Margaret Bardolph至少有2个儿子。他去世于1372年在英国薩默塞特斯塔維爾，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Hody
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Caitlin, Conor, Declan, Thomas Patrick.
Irish (Louth): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEochaidh ‘descendant of Eochaidh’, a variant of Eachaidh, a personal name meaning ‘horseman’ (a derivative of each ‘horse’).
Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead named Hoøy from Old Norse hór ‘high’ + ey ‘island’. This surname is very rare in Norway.

variants.name: Hoy
meanings: English (Essex): nickname for a sailor, from Middle Dutch hoey ‘cargo ship’.
Danish (Høy): nickname for a tall person, from høj ‘high’.

variants.name: Haughey
meanings: Irish (Tyrone): variant of Hoey from Ó hEachaidh. Compare McCaughey .

variants.name: Haugh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Connor, Eamon.
Irish (Clare): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEachach ‘descendant of Eochu’, a personal name which is probably a pet form of Eochaidh, Eachaidh (see Hoey ).

Scottish and English: from northern Middle English and Older Scots halh, haugh ‘a piece of flat alluvial land by the side of a river, typically one deposited in a bend’, also ‘river-meadow land’; either topographic for someone who lived near such a feature or habitational for someone who came from one of several places in Scotland and northern England called Haugh.

variants.name: Haughn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, probably a diminutive of Eachaidh (see Hoey ).

variants.name: Xu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Wei, Jing, Jian, Ming, Li, Yi, Hui, Bin, Gang, Hong, Ping, Jin, Min, Chong, You, Chang, Hu, Neng, Shen, Yiming, Yiping.  Vietnamese Long, Hao, Lan, Hai, Bian, Buu, Cong, Dai, Mai, Nu, Quan, Thi.

Mandarin form of the surname 徐, meaning ‘gradually, slowly’ in Chinese: (i) from Xu (徐), the name of a state (covering present-day Jiangsu and Anhui province) originally granted to Ruo Mu, son of an official called Bo Yi during the reign of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). The state was destroyed by the state of Chu during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) but later restored and eventually annexed by the state of Wu in 512  BC . After its annihilation, the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) borne by the descendants of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). The Xu family was one of the six clans of the Shang dynasty that were sent to the state of Lu after the annihilation of the Shang dynasty. (iii) surname borne by descendants of Xu Zhi Gao (徐知誥), an alternative name of Li Sheng, the founder of the state of Southern Tang (937–975  AD ). (iv) a surname of the Xu (徐) family from the Di ethnic group in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Hody was born from 1330 to 1332, in Hampshire, England, his father, Richard Hody, was 23 and his mother, Margaret Wormegay, was 17. He had at least 2 sons with Margaret Bardolph. He died in 1372, in Stawell, Somerset, England, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Hody est né du 1330 au 1332, à Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Hody, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Margaret Wormegay, avait 17 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Margaret Bardolph. Il est décédé en 1372, à Stawell, Somerset, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Hody von 1330 bis 1332 in Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Hody 23 und seine Mutter Margaret Wormegay 17. Er hatte mit Margaret Bardolph mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1372 in Stawell, Somerset, England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Hody è nato il da 1330 a 1332 nel Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Richard Hody, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Wormegay, aveva 17 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Margaret Bardolph. Morì nel 1372, nel Stawell, Somerset, England, e fu sepolto a England.

ja.Hampshire, England: イギリスハンプシュアイア
ja.Stowell, Somerset, England, United Kingd: イギリスイギリスソメルセトストウエル
ja.Stawell, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトスタウエル
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Hodyがから1330年まで1332年にイギリスハンプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard Hodyは23歳，母Margaret Wormegayは17歳であった。彼はMargaret Bardolphとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1372年にイギリスソメルセトスタウエルで死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Hody(이)가 1330년 부터 1332년 까지 영국 함프실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Hody(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Wormegay(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Margaret Bardolph(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1372년 영국 서머세트 스타웰에서 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

mn.Stowell, Somerset, England, United Kingd: Stowell, Bro an Hañv, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Hody nasceu de 1330 a 1332, em Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Hody, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Wormegay, tinha 17 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Margaret Bardolph. Ele faleceu em 1372, em Stawell, Somerset, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Hody родился с 1330 по 1332 в местности хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Hody было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Wormegay, 17. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Margaret Bardolph. Он умер в 1372 г. в местности ставелл, сомирсет, Англия и был похоронен в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Hody nació desde 1330 hasta 1332, en Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Hody, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Margaret Wormegay, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Margaret Bardolph. Murió en 1372, en Stawell, Somerset, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra.
